reasoning: Query asks for cherry blossom spots across Tohoku (6 prefectures), but tool was called only for Akita. Per RULE C, authoritative Akita data scores 3 on affected dims, but the primary shortfall is retrieval — only 1/6 Tohoku prefectures covered. Spanish-language query got English/Japanese response (RULE D), dropping constraint_handling by 1 from an otherwise 4. RULE B: single worst dim is intent (regional scope missed).
missing_data: Aomori (Hirosaki Castle), Iwate, Miyagi, Yamagata, Fukushima cherry blossom spots; sakura_meisho_100 filter not applied; response in wrong language (Spanish query → en/ja output)
improvement_hint: Fan out to all 6 Tohoku prefectures when 'Tohoku' toponym is detected; apply kind filter sakura_meisho_100 to surface cherry blossom specific spots; return descriptions in query language (es).

reasoning: Tool correctly called get_local_specialty for Niigata sake. Response returns 24 items including famous brands (越乃寒梅 Koshi no Kanbai, 八海山 Hakkaisan, 久保田 Kubota) from authoritative METI/Wikidata sources with English names. One caveat: query asks for 'major sake breweries' as physical venues but response lists brands/designations rather than brewery addresses/visit info — reduces practical_usefulness and specificity by 1.
missing_data: Physical brewery addresses and visitor information (tours, opening hours) for major breweries like Niigata City breweries district; remaining 20+ items not shown in truncated response
improvement_hint: Cross-link sake_brand/sake_brewery wikidata entities with get_spots venue data to surface brewery physical locations alongside the designation records.

reasoning: Query asks for ski resorts WITH hot springs (onsen) near Hakuba in French. Tool returns Hakuba spots including the top ski resort (Happo-One), but no onsen/hot spring spots appear in the truncated response — the dual constraint (ski + onsen) is only half met. RULE D: French query got Japanese/English data, dropping constraint_handling by 1. RULE C: authoritative Hakuba ski data present scores 3, not 2.
missing_data: Onsen facilities in Hakuba area (Hakuba Happo Onsen, Mimizuku no Yu, Garnet Onsen etc.); no hot spring spots tagged in response; French-language descriptions absent
improvement_hint: When query contains both ski and onsen constraints, call get_spots filtering on both ski_resort and onsen kinds, or call twice and merge; add onsen_bath kind to Hakuba venue data.

reasoning: Query asks for Kansai autumn foliage spots — Kansai spans Kyoto, Osaka, Hyogo, Nara, Shiga, Wakayama, Mie. Tool only called for Kyoto. More critically, no foliage-specific kind filter (koyo/autumn_leaves) was applied, so results are general Kyoto spots (Nijo Castle, To-ji) not specifically autumn foliage. Regional scope is 1/7 prefectures covered — primary failure is retrieval (RULE B single dim). Per RULE C, Kyoto data is real and authoritative, so dims drop to 3 not 2.
missing_data: Osaka (Minoo), Nara (Yoshino), Hyogo (Kobe foliage parks), Shiga (Eikan-do equiv), Mie, Wakayama foliage spots; koyo/autumn_foliage kind filter not applied
improvement_hint: Detect 'Kansai' as multi-prefecture region and fan out to all 7 prefectures; apply autumn foliage kind tags (koyo, autumn_garden) as filter when seasonal foliage is the explicit intent.

reasoning: Arabic query for famous cherry blossom spots in Kansai. Tool returned Nara spots which is one Kansai prefecture — partial hit. Yoshino (吉野山) with sakura_meisho_100 tag is at top of results, highly relevant. RULE D: Arabic query got ja/en response — constraint_handling drops 1. RULE C: authoritative Nara data with actual sakura_meisho_100 spots scores 3 on regional coverage dims. Better than L2-09 because at least sakura spots appear.
missing_data: Other Kansai prefectures (Osaka Mint Bureau, Kyoto, Hyogo, etc.) for cherry blossoms; Arabic-language descriptions absent
improvement_hint: Detect 'Kansai' (كانساي) and fan out to all 7 prefectures; sakura_meisho_100 filter was implicitly applied (Yoshino surfaced) — document this behavior; add Arabic description translation support.

reasoning: Query asks for dark sky / stargazing spots specifically on Okinawa's remote islands (離島). Tool returned Okinawa prefecture-level spots — leading results are Nakagusuku Castle, Tamaudun mausoleum (Naha), cultural heritage sites, not islands with dark skies. Kumejima appears but as a municipality entry not specifically as a stargazing spot. No dark_sky or island-isolation kind filter applied. Primary failure: retrieval — wrong kind of content surfaced for this specific intent.
missing_data: Dark sky designated spots; Iriomote, Ishigaki, Miyako, Yonaguni, Kerama islands specific stargazing sites; no dark_sky_island or remote_island kind tags used
improvement_hint: Add dark_sky kind tag to remote Okinawan islands; when query contains stargazing + remote island intent, filter by island + low light pollution proxies; municipality-level filter to specific islands (石垣市 etc.) would help.

reasoning: Thai-language query for rural Hokkaido onsen accommodations. Tool returned Hokkaido hotels including excellent 日本秘湯を守る会 (remote/hidden onsen ryokan association) entries which directly match 'rural onsen' intent — 知内温泉, 銀婚湯, 凌雲閣, 湯元湧駒荘 are genuinely rural. However ANA Holiday Inn Sapporo (urban city hotel) also appears, diluting rural focus. RULE D: Thai query got Japanese/English response — constraint_handling drops 1. Per RULE C, rural onsen data is present but mixed with urban hotels.
missing_data: Thai-language descriptions; filtering to exclude urban Sapporo hotels; more rural remote onsen ryokan across Hokkaido regions (Daisetsuzan, Shiretoko area)
improvement_hint: When 'rural/countryside' (田舎/ชนบท) constraint is present, filter out city-center hotel type entries; prioritize hito_yu_kai source which reliably indicates remote onsen ryokan; return descriptions in query language (th).

reasoning: Vietnamese query for islands of the Seto Inland Sea (瀬戸内海). Seto Inland Sea spans Kagawa, Hiroshima, Okayama, Hyogo, Yamaguchi, Ehime. Tool only called for Kagawa. Results show Ritsurin Garden (mainland Takamatsu), Mannō Lake, Marugame Castle — none of these are Seto Inland Sea islands. Famous art islands (Naoshima, Teshima, Shodoshima) absent. Primary retrieval failure: wrong scope and wrong kind of content.
missing_data: Naoshima (直島), Teshima (豊島), Shodoshima (小豆島), Awajishima, Ōshima; island kind filter not applied; Hiroshima prefecture (Miyajima/Itsukushima) absent; Vietnamese-language descriptions absent
improvement_hint: Detect 'Seto Inland Sea' as multi-prefecture maritime region; apply island kind filter; add Setouchi-specific region tag spanning relevant prefectures; Naoshima/Teshima art island data needs enrichment.

reasoning: Query asks for indigo dyeing workshops in Tokushima. Tool correctly called get_local_specialty with craft filter. Returned 3 items: 阿波正藍しじら織 (indigo shijira weave, METI designated), 大谷焼 (ceramics — not directly indigo), 阿波和紙 (indigo-dyed washi). The core indigo textile craft is present (item 1), washi-indigo is tangential (item 3), ceramics are off-topic (item 2). Count of 3 is thin for a 'workshop' query. RULE B: single worst dim is coverage (G). No dedicated 阿波藍 indigo-dyeing experience venues returned.
missing_data: Dedicated 阿波藍 indigo dyeing experience venues/workshops (体験工房); 藍住町 indigo production area context; more specific workshop addresses and booking info
improvement_hint: Add 'indigo_dyeing' experience kind tag to Tokushima specialty venues; cross-link METI records with get_spots to surface associated workshop venues in 阿波藍 production areas.

reasoning: German query for shukubo (temple lodging) on Mount Koya. Tool called get_hotels for Wakayama and response correctly surfaces multiple 高野山宿坊協会 (Koyasan Shukubo Association) confirmed entries — 安養院, 一乗院, 惠光院, 北室院, 熊谷寺 — all genuine Koyasan shukubo with Japanese descriptions, phone numbers, and official URLs. This is an excellent tool call. Minor caveat: descriptions are Japanese-only (German speaker gets ja descriptions), and coordinates are null for all shukubo entries, reducing practical_usefulness slightly.
missing_data: German-language descriptions; GPS coordinates for shukubo to aid navigation; English descriptions sparse
improvement_hint: Add coordinate data to Koyasan shukubo entries (many have known addresses that can be geocoded); generate German translations for shukubo descriptions.

reasoning: Query for samurai districts outside Kyoto. Tool correctly used search_area with '武家屋敷' returning 153 results. Top results include Shimane (Matsue area buke yashiki), Fukui (旧内山家), Akita (角館武家屋敷 with preservation_district designation) — all genuinely outside Kyoto. Kakunodate entry has rich description and Q850649 Important Preservation District designation. One caveat: first two results lack name_en translations, and the Tokyo 武家屋敷門 result appears (technically outside Kyoto but not 'off the beaten path'). Overall strong response.
missing_data: Chiran (知覧) samurai district in Kagoshima; Kitsuki (杵築) in Oita; some entries lack English names for agent synthesis
improvement_hint: Boost buke_yashiki+preservation_district combination in ranking; add 'off-the-beaten-path' proxy signals via crowd_band=low filter when query contains that phrase.

reasoning: Indonesian query for summer festivals in Akita. Tool correctly called get_festivals with lang=id returning Indonesian translations. However, only 3 items returned (count=3) and notably the most famous Akita summer festival — 竿燈まつり (Kanto Festival, UNESCO) — is absent. The 3 results are Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties (Dainichido Bugaku, Kakunodate Festival, Tsuchizaki Shrine Festival) which are legitimate but not specifically 'summer festivals' (dates missing). Coverage is sparse. RULE B worst dim: coverage (G).
missing_data: 竿燈まつり (Akita Kanto Festival, one of Tohoku's three major summer festivals); 大曲の花火大会 (major fireworks); summer-specific date filtering; hanabi/natsu_matsuri kind tags
wrong_data: Date '1705-01-01' for Tsuchizaki festival is clearly a data artifact (Wikidata founding date, not festival date)
improvement_hint: Integrate hanabi/fireworks festival data from scraped_schema_event source for Akita summer events; fix date field — founding year should not be surfaced as event date; add Kanto Festival UNESCO entry.

reasoning: Korean query for small fishing port towns in the San'in region. San'in spans Shimane and Tottori. Tool only called for Shimane, missing Tottori. More critically, results show Lake Shinji, Matsue Castle, Iwami Ginzan Silver Mine — famous attractions but not 'small fishing port towns' (漁港町). No port/harbor/fishing-village kind tags applied. Primary failure: retrieval of wrong content type. RULE C: authoritative Shimane data present, dims score 3 not 2.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture (Uradome coast, fishing ports); fishing village/harbor kind tags (港町, 漁村); Yunotsu onsen port town; Hamada fishing port; Oki Islands fishing communities; Korean-language descriptions
improvement_hint: Add fishing_port / harbor_town kind tags to coastal Shimane/Tottori scrape data; when San'in is detected fan out to both Shimane and Tottori; filter results by coastal/maritime kinds when query specifies fishing ports.

reasoning: Query for washi paper making experience in Gifu. Tool called get_local_specialty with craft filter for Gifu returning 8 items. However leading results are Mino Ware (ceramics), Hida Shunkei (lacquerware), Nagiso Rokuro Zaiku (woodwork) — not washi. 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi) should be a key result for Gifu washi but only Ichinoi wood carving is shown in truncation. The query specifically asks about washi (and experiences/workshops), but the response lacks washi-specific filtering. RULE C: authoritative data present, scope mismatch scores 3 not 2.
missing_data: 美濃和紙 (Mino Washi, METI designated) — the most relevant item; 美濃市 washi experience centers/workshops; the 8-item list likely contains it but ranking puts unrelated crafts first
improvement_hint: Apply text matching to surface washi/paper items first when query specifically mentions washi; add experience/workshop venue cross-links; ensure 美濃和紙 is in Gifu specialty data.

reasoning: Spanish query for fishing villages on the Noto Peninsula. Tool returned Ishikawa spots but results are dominated by Kanazawa city attractions (Kanazawa Castle, Kenroku-en, Oyama Shrine) — not Noto Peninsula fishing villages. The Noto Peninsula (能登半島) is a distinct sub-region of Ishikawa with unique fishing communities. No municipality filter for Noto area towns (Wajima, Suzu, Noto-cho) was applied. Primary failure: retrieval — wrong geographic sub-region and wrong content type. RULE D: Spanish query → ja/en output, constraint_handling -1.
missing_data: Noto Peninsula specific spots: Wajima (輪島) morning market, Suzu salt fields, Ōgacho fishing ports, kiriko festival spots; municipality-level filtering for Noto area; Spanish-language descriptions
improvement_hint: Detect 'Noto Peninsula' (能登半島) as a sub-prefecture region and call get_spots with municipality filters for Noto-area towns (輪島市, 珠洲市, 能登町); add fishing_port/fishing_village kind tags.

reasoning: Query for lesser-known sand dunes beyond Tottori. Tool used search_area for '砂丘' returning 91 results. Top results include Tottori (expected, appropriately surfaced), then Onjuku Beach (Chiba), Ishikari sand dune museum (Hokkaido), Fukiagehama (Kagoshima) — genuinely diverse national coverage. One caveat: an Ishikari Museum appears as sand_dune (it's a museum about dunes, not an actual dune visit spot) slightly reducing specificity. The query's 'not just Tottori' constraint is reasonably met by the diverse hits.
missing_data: Nakatajima sand dunes (浜松市, 中田島砂丘); Sarfutsu dunes (Hokkaido); some results may be tangential museum rather than dune visit spots
improvement_hint: Distinguish sand_dune as a place-to-visit vs. a museum-about-dunes in kind tagging; add Nakatajima (静岡) and other nationally designated sand dune spots to coverage.

reasoning: Chinese query for traditional crafts of the San'in region. Tool called get_local_specialty for Shimane only (San'in = Shimane + Tottori), missing Tottori. Results show 4 crafts with excellent Chinese translations: 石見焼 (Iwami pottery), 石州和紙 (Sekishu Washi, UNESCO ICH), 雲州そろばん (abacus), 出雲石燈ろう (stone lanterns) — all genuine Shimane METI crafts with high-quality Chinese descriptions. Coverage gap (Tottori crafts like 因州和紙 absent) is the only weakness. RULE C: 3 on coverage dims.
missing_data: Tottori prefecture crafts (因州和紙 Inshu Washi, 弓浜絣 Yumihama Gasuri cotton); San'in should span both Shimane and Tottori
improvement_hint: Detect 'San'in' (山陰) as multi-prefecture region (Shimane + Tottori) and fan out accordingly; this is a consistent gap across San'in queries in this batch.

reasoning: Query for lavender fields in Hokkaido beyond Furano. Tool returned general Hokkaido spots — Shōwa-shinzan volcano, Sapporo Clock Tower, Matsumae Castle — none are lavender fields. The lavender/flower-garden kind tag (lavender field) is entirely absent from the returned results. Famous lavender spots like Tomita Farm (富田ファーム, Nakafurano) or Zerubu no Oka don't appear. Primary retrieval failure: no lavender-specific content in general get_spots response for Hokkaido.
missing_data: Lavender field spots: 富田ファーム (Tomita Farm, Nakafurano), ファーム富田, ぜるぶの丘, カントリーガーデン; no lavender_field kind tag applied; Furano/Biei area municipal filter not used
improvement_hint: Add lavender_field kind tag to Furano/Biei area spots; when query specifies flower type, apply kind filter rather than returning general prefecture spots; Tomita Farm is a high-prominence Hokkaido attraction missing from the corpus.

reasoning: Filipino (Tagalog) query for 'traditional towns in Kyushu' — literally asks for 'tradisyonal na bayan ng kape sa Kyushu' which translates to 'traditional coffee towns in Kyushu'. However given context (九州 古い町並み = old townscapes in Kyushu), the likely intent is traditional old townscapes. Tool returned Oita spots: Usuki Castle, Usa Jingū, Oka Castle — none are specifically 'old townscapes' (町並み). Oita is just 1 of 7 Kyushu prefectures. Tagalog query → ja/en response (RULE D, -1 constraint). The response barely addresses the traditional townscape intent.
missing_data: Actual historic townscape spots: Yanagawa (柳川), Arita (有田), Hita (日田 豆田町), Usuki historic district; Nagasaki, Kumamoto, Fukuoka, Kagoshima, Miyazaki, Saga prefectures absent; preservation_district kind filter not applied
improvement_hint: Detect Kyushu as multi-prefecture region and fan out to all 7 prefectures; apply preservation_district/jokamachi/kominka kind filter for traditional townscape queries; Tagalog language support in descriptions needed.

reasoning: Query for kominka (old farmhouse) stays in Hokuriku region. Hokuriku = Ishikawa + Toyama + Fukui (sometimes Niigata). Tool called for Ishikawa only. Results show onsen ryokan (日本秘湯) and city hotels — no kominka-type lodging. Query specifically asks for 古民家 (old traditional farmhouse accommodation) but the hotel data has no kominka type/tag. ANA Crowne Plaza and AB Hotel are clearly not kominka. The lodging type taxonomy lacks kominka as a category.
missing_data: Kominka-type lodging in Ishikawa (Noto Peninsula machiya, Gassho-zukuri in Toyama/Gifu border area); Toyama, Fukui coverage absent; kominka lodging_type not in hotel taxonomy
improvement_hint: Add kominka/machiya as lodging_type in hotel data; fan out to Toyama and Fukui for Hokuriku region queries; Shirakawa-go adjacent kominka stays (Toyama side) are a notable gap in Hokuriku coverage.

reasoning: Query for mountain shrines on the Kii Peninsula. Tool called get_japan_heritage for Wakayama returning 7 items with top_canonical_heritage including Kongōbu-ji Temple (Koyasan), Kumano Kodō UNESCO pilgrimage route, Kumano Sanzan (three grand shrines). These are precisely the most authoritative mountain shrine/sacred mountain responses possible for the Kii Peninsula. Tool scope note correctly references pilgrimage routes and UNESCO WHS. One caveat: Mie prefecture (also Kii Peninsula — Ise Jingū area) not covered, and heritage_designations_labels show 'Q11633166' unresolved QID.
missing_data: Mie prefecture Kii Peninsula content (Ise Jingū, Nachi Taisha in Mie side); unresolved heritage label Q11633166 should display as text
wrong_data: heritage_designations_labels contains raw QID 'Q11633166' instead of resolved label text
improvement_hint: Resolve all heritage QID labels before output; extend Kii Peninsula queries to include Mie prefecture; the scope note about pilgrimage routes is helpful — maintain it.

reasoning: Query for summer fireworks along the Sea of Japan coast. Tool called get_festivals for Niigata (one prefecture of the Japan Sea coast, which also spans Akita, Yamagata, Ishikawa, Fukui, Tottori, Shimane). Niigata coverage is actually excellent — 129 events returned, including 長岡まつり大花火大会 (one of Japan's top fireworks festivals) with dates and descriptions. However the query scope is the entire Japan Sea coast, not just Niigata. Coverage dim drops by 1 for missing other Sea of Japan prefectures' fireworks.
missing_data: Other Japan Sea coast fireworks: Akita 大曲の花火, Yamagata Tsuruoka fireworks, Fukui fireworks, Tottori fireworks; 'Sea of Japan coast' as region not detected
improvement_hint: Detect 'Sea of Japan coast' (日本海沿岸) as multi-prefecture region spanning Niigata through to Shimane/Tottori; the Niigata festival data quality is excellent — replicate it for other Sea of Japan prefectures.

reasoning: Query for bamboo crafts in Oita. Tool correctly called get_local_specialty with craft filter. Returns 2 items: 博多織 (Hakata weave — only partly in Oita, primarily Fukuoka) and 別府竹細工 (Beppu Bamboo Crafts) — the latter is exactly what was asked for with detailed English description, METI authority, and specific production area. One caveat: Hakata Ori is primarily a Fukuoka craft (listed as Oita secondary production area), and the low count (2) is sparse for Oita crafts overall. But the bamboo craft intent is well-served.
missing_data: Other Oita crafts may exist; Beppu bamboo workshop venue/experience locations not linked; 日田下駄 (Hita wooden clogs) absent if it exists in corpus
improvement_hint: Cross-link Beppu Bamboo Crafts METI record with workshop/museum venue data in Beppu; check if additional Oita traditional crafts are missing from METI data (日田下駄, Usuki stone Buddha area crafts).

reasoning: Query for cycling routes through rural Shikoku. Shikoku has 4 prefectures (Ehime, Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa). Tool only called for Ehime. Within Ehime results, Ōmishima island appears with cycling_route kind tag — directly relevant. However other 3 Shikoku prefectures are absent, and general castle/heritage spots dominate over cycling-specific content. The Shimanami Kaidō (しまなみ海道) which runs through Ehime and is Japan's most famous cycling route is not prominently featured. RULE C: partial authoritative data scores 3.
missing_data: Kochi, Tokushima, Kagawa cycling routes; Shimanami Kaidō prominence boost; 四万十川 (Shimanto river cycling); rural cycling path data largely absent
improvement_hint: Fan out to all 4 Shikoku prefectures when 'Shikoku' is detected; apply cycling_route kind filter to surface relevant spots; Shimanami Kaidō is a major cycling destination needing dedicated enrichment.

reasoning: Query for traditional kabuki outside Tokyo and Kyoto. Tool called get_traditional_arts without a kabuki filter, returning 183 items. 歌舞伎 (Kabuki) does appear as item 2 (UNESCO ICH Q199701) but is a generic nationwide record with no geographic specificity. The response lists Bunraku, Kabuki, Chakkirako, Dainichido Bugaku in order — kabuki is present but lacks venue/location detail for 'where to see it outside Tokyo/Kyoto'. No regional kabuki theaters (Kotohira Kabuki in Kagawa 金毘羅, Chichibu Yomatsuri in Saitama) surfaced. RULE B: data present but useful venue-level content buried or missing.
missing_data: Regional kabuki performance venues: 旧金毘羅大芝居 (Kagawa, oldest kabuki theater), 秩父夜祭歌舞伎, Kami-za theaters in regional cities; geographic filtering to exclude Tokyo/Kyoto; venue addresses and performance schedule
improvement_hint: Add regional kabuki theater/venue data with kabuki_theater kind tag; when 'outside Tokyo/Kyoto' constraint is present, apply negative prefecture filters; link get_traditional_arts kabuki record to specific performance venues via get_spots.
